rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NORMAN LLOYD PETERSEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On Disbursements please explain all entries regarding legal fees and provide the date and case number of the court order authorizing the fees. If there is no court order authorizing the fees please explain the disbursement, calculate any interest owned, pay back the trust, and file a receipt. 2) On Disbursements please explain the conservator fees and expenses and file receipts for each entry. 3) On Disbursement please file a receipt for entry 12/16/201 Fruitvale Healthcare room and board for $10,980.00. 4) Account ends 12/31/2021 with approximately $102,931.72 in assets. Petitioner is requesting to terminate the trust. No legal authority or probate codes cited in support of terminating the trust. 5) Trustee requests $11,589.00 be paid to court-appointed counsel Christopher Jew without joining in the petition or timesheets and Mr. Jew has filed a petition for fees in the conservatorship with a hearing date set for 07/11/2022. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Final Accounts From Deceased Executor
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL JOHN BERNHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The estate funds on hand in the amount of $770,560.44 have been held in a non-interest-bearing account since July 2021. Probate Code section 9652. 2) The administrator refuses to observe the FDIC limit. The estate funds in excess of $520,000 are uninsured. Probate Code sections 46, 9700, & 10533(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Obtain Possession And Title To Real And Personal Property Under Probate Code Section 850
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Rebekah Mark, the decedent's former spouse, objects to the estate's interest in the Guggenheim Life Insurance Contract, stating that the Guggenheim asset is a life insurance policy within the meaning of Probate Code section 5040(e). Rebekah Mark, as the designated beneficiary of the Guggenheim life insurance policy, requests confirmation that the estate has no interest in the policy. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KANTABEN IL PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On the petition page 2 please explain the relation of Manilal Patel and Ishverbhai N. Patel to Decedent. 2) Please give required 15 days' notice to Manilal Patel with the required language found in the 10th general order found on the court's website. 3) Please address Local Rule 14.26 A. and B. 4) Please file the deed for 143 8th Avenue, bank statements for the alleged accounts, and documents showing the ownership interest in Oceanview Motel LLC. 5) Please prove the assets are community property. 6) Please explain if/how In re Marriage of Moore and In re Marriage of Marsden apply to this petition and prove the calculation of community property interest. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 05/01/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 05/15/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PHILIP MILAM WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petitioner seeking appointment should appear to clarify if the precedeased brothers were survived by issue. The "supplement" that puported to amend item 8 of the petition only listed the two predeceased brothers but did not identify their issue. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions of "supplement" vs. "amendment" and 7.53 re notice requirement for an amending pleading.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: 006Deeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANG WU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
